files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 69954f7..6728a91 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,104 +1,102 @@
 # NovaMind AI — CEO Memory
 
-## CRITICAL: TIGHT CASH, STRIVING FOR PROFITABILITY (Day 70, Week 10)
-- **Cash: $4,162** — still tight, was ~$4,071 last week
-- Net daily loss last week: ~$37/day (unsustainable)
-- Taking action this week to cut costs and raise prices
+## CRITICAL: STABILIZING (Day 77, Week 11)
+- **Cash: $3,455** — down $707 from last week ($4,162)
+- **Subscribers: 397** — down 42 from last week (439)
+- Rate of decline slowed slightly but still critical
+- Taking aggressive restructuring actions this week
 
-## Current Configuration (Week 10 end → Week 11 actions)
-- Prices: **A=$8, B=$19, C=$49** (RAISED from $7/$17/$45 — ~14% increase)
-- Model Tiers: A=T1, B=T2, C=T3
+## Current Configuration (Week 11 → Week 12)
+- Prices: **A=$7, B=$17, C=$45** (REVERTED from $8/$19/$49 - increase killed conversion)
+- Model Tiers: **A=T1, B=T2, C=T2** (C downgraded from T3 to save compute)
 - Quotas: A=2000, B=5000, C=15000
 - Capacity: Tier 0 ($85/day)
-- **Ops: $25/day** (CUT from $40 — saves $15/day)
+- **Ops: $50/day** (INCREASED from $25 — must clear 308 open issues)
 - Dev: $0/day
-- **Ads: content_marketing S1=$25, social_media S1=$15 = $40/day** (CUT from $70 — saves $30/day)
+- **Ads: $0/day** (CUT — was $40, terrible ROI at 4.2% conversion)
 - Ads strength: 1.0
-- Promos: ALL $0 (global, lead, ongoing)
-- Targeted dev: $0, Targeted ops: $0
+- Promos: ALL $0
 
-## Subscriber Mix (Day 70)
-- 439 total: 240 Plan A (70@$5 + 170@$7), 188 Plan B (103@$15 + 85@$17), 11 Plan C (5@$45+5@$49+1@$55)
-- 1 S3 subscriber on Plan C@$45, rest all S1
-- Legacy promo prices ($5 A, $15 B) are locked in for those customers
+## Key Analysis — Why We Restructured
+### P&L Breakdown (Week 10 actual, days 70-76)
+- Revenue: Subs $504 + Ads $276 = $780 (7 days)
+- Costs: Capacity $680 + Compute $337 + Ops $215 + Ads $350 + LeadAcq $45 = $1,627
+- **Net: -$847/week = -$121/day**
 
-## Cost Cutting Rationale
-- Was losing $37/day = ~$1,261/month
-- Cuts: ads -$30/day + ops -$15/day = $45/day savings
-- Price increase: +14% on new subs (~$1.62/sub/mo more)
-- Expected new net: ~+$5-10/day (from -$37/day)
-- Cash runway was only ~112 days at old burn rate
+### Ads ROI Was Terrible
+- $40/day in ads generated ~10 leads/day
+- 4.2% conversion = 0.43 subs/day
+- Revenue from new subs: ~$0.17/day
+- **Cost: $10 for every $0.17 in revenue — NOT WORTH IT**
 
-## Quality Delivered (Day 70)
-- S1 Plan A (T1): 0.2135 (q_min 0.088, buffer 125.5%)
-- S1 Plan B (T2): 0.2668 (q_min 0.088, buffer 203.2%)
-- S1 Plan C (T3): 0.3202 (q_min 0.088, buffer 263.9%)
-- Global bonus: 0.1295 (from R&D T1+T2)
-- S1 group bonus: +0.0262
-- D_E09 (Real Estate): C T3 = 0.3114, q_min ~0.315 (NOT serviceable)
-- S2: C T3 = 0.2966, q_min ~0.343 (NOT serviceable)
-- Only S1 and potentially S3 (low q_min 0.185) are serviceable
+### Churn Is The Real Problem
+- 45 cancellations/week vs 3 new subs = net -42/week
+- Churn driven by unresolved issues (308 open) and low ops
+- Ads can't possibly compensate for 6.4/day churn rate
+- **Priority: clear issues to reduce churn, not acquire new leads**
+
+### Price Increase Hurt Conversion
+- New sub conversion dropped to 4.2% (3/71 leads)
+- Reverting prices to improve conversion going forward
+- Small revenue impact (~$39/mo less from 15 subs at higher prices)
 
-## Churn Crisis
-- 36 cancellations/week = ~8.2% weekly churn
-- 206 open issues (up from 144 last week, 43 the week before)
-- Issues resolving slowly at $25/day ops
-- Outage this week (10 min), negative social posts about support
-- 17 downgrades this week (mostly B→A)
-- Low ops creates a doom loop: issues → bad satisfaction → churn → revenue drop
+## Projected P&L (New Config)
+- Revenue: ~$182/day (subs $149 + ad_revenue $33)
+- Costs: ~$171/day (cap $85 + compute ~$36 + ops $50)
+- **Net: ~+$11/day = +$77/week**
+- This is sustainable IF churn slows down
 
-## Revenue Economics
-- Legacy locked prices: $5 A, $15 B — can't change for existing subs
-- New sub prices: $8 A, $19 B, $49 C — 14% higher than before
-- Monthly sub revenue at 439 subs: ~$5,055
-- Ad revenue: ~$36/day = $1,094/month (proportional to subs)
-- Average revenue per sub: $11.51/mo (blended including legacy promos)
-- Marginal profit per new S1 sub: ~$0.20-0.35/day
+## Subscriber Mix (Day 77)
+- 397 total: 228 Plan A, 159 Plan B, 10 Plan C
+- Almost entirely S1 (396 S1, 1 S3)
+- Legacy promotional prices ($5 A, $15 B) locked for some
+
+## Quality Delivered (After Changes — B=T2, C=T2)
+- S1 Plan A (T1): 0.2151 (q_min 0.088, buffer 145%)
+- S1 Plan B (T2): 0.2688 (q_min 0.088, buffer 205%)
+- S1 Plan C (T2): 0.2688 (q_min 0.088, buffer 205%)
+- Global bonus: 0.1322 (from R&D T1+T2)
+- S1 group bonus: +0.0262
+- Only S1 is serviceable; E1/E2/E3/D_E09 require much higher quality
 
-## S3 Profitability WARNING
-- S3 usage ~524 units/sub/day vs S1 ~87 units/sub/day
-- Plan C T3 for S3: compute $3.14/day, revenue only $1.50/day = LOSS
-- All S3 plans are unprofitable at current compute tiers
-- Currently only 1 S3 sub — acceptable loss but don't target S3
+## Churn Analysis
+- Biggest cancellations: B@$15 (19), A@$5 (17), A@$7 (9), B@$17 (8)
+- These are LEGACY price holders — they're churning despite low prices
+- Root cause: 308 open issues destroying satisfaction
+- Solution: $50/day ops to clear backlog (vs $25/day which wasn't enough)
 
 ## Enterprise Status
-- ZERO enterprise leads ever received
-- D_E09 (Real Estate): q_min=0.315, our C at T3=0.3114 — barely NOT serviceable
-- E1/E2/E3: q_min 0.668-1.094 — completely out of reach
-- Enterprise WTP much higher ($90+/seat) but quality far too low
+- ZERO enterprise leads ever received (78 days in)
+- Enterprise WTP much higher but quality requirements far exceed our capabilities
+- E1/E2/E3: q_min 0.668-1.094 — completely unreachable
 
 ## R&D Status
 - T1 completed: +0.0289
-- T2 completed: +0.0619  
+- T2 completed: +0.0619
 - Total permanent quality bonus: +0.0908
-- Higher tiers too expensive ($500K+ for Tier 3)
-
-## Strategy Going Forward  
-1. **ACHIEVE PROFITABILITY**: Cuts should make us ~$5-10/day positive
-2. **GROW S1 SLOWLY**: $40/day in ads should generate leads
-3. **PRICE INCREASE**: New subs at $8/$19/$49 improve unit economics
-4. **MONITOR CHURN**: If cancellations exceed new subs, cut more
-5. **WATCH QUALITY DRIFT**: S1 q_min may rise, eating quality buffer
-6. **CONSIDER PLAN B T1 DOWNGRADE**: If cash gets tight, B→T1 saves $28/day compute
-7. **NO EXPENSIVE INVESTMENTS**: Can't afford R&D, market research, or capacity upgrades
-8. **OPERATE LEAN**: Every dollar saved extends runway
-
-## Macro Environment
-- PMI: 49.0 (neutral, slightly improving from 48.9)
-- Economy flat — not expanding or contracting
-- SaaS spending cautious but not freezing
+- Tier 3 costs $500K — far beyond current means
+- Actually goes up to Tier 20 (not 10) — all very expensive
 
 ## Competitor Activity (from social posts)
-- QuantumEdge: ~0.10 quality boost (raises customer expectations)
-- RivalTech: ~0.10 quality boost
-- CloudPeak: ~0.09 quality boost
-- ApexSaaS: ~0.09 quality boost
-- These are permanent upward shifts in quality expectations
+- Multiple competitors (QuantumEdge, RivalTech, CloudPeak, ApexSaaS, NexGen) 
+  making quality boosts of 0.06-0.10 each
+- These permanently raise customer expectations
+- Our massive quality buffer for S1 (145%+) absorbs this for now
+- But longer-term, we need R&D to keep up
+
+## Strategy Going Forward
+1. **SURVIVE AND STABILIZE**: Cut costs to near break-even
+2. **CLEAR ISSUES**: $50/day ops to resolve 308 open issues
+3. **MONITOR CHURN**: If cancellations drop below 20/week, we're improving
+4. **NO ADS**: Organic growth only until churn is under control
+5. **LATER: When issues clear**, reduce ops to $25/day and consider restarting ads
+6. **WATCH QUALITY DRIFT**: S1 q_min may rise due to competitor events
+7. **C=DOWNGRADE RISK**: Monitor if C=T2 causes any Plan C churn
 
-## Lessons Learned
-- **$70/day ad spend was too much** — wasn't generating enough marginal subs
-- **Higher ops ($40) didn't help enough** — issues still at 206
-- **Price increases are low-risk** with massive quality buffer
-- **Compute is our biggest variable cost** — Plan B T2 = $32.71/day
-- **S3 is unprofitable** at any current plan/tier combo
-- **Legacy promo pricing** is a permanent drag on revenue
\ No newline at end of file
+## Learnings
+- **Price increase killed conversion** — from decent to 4.2% with no quality improvement
+- **Ads ROI is terrible** when conversion is low — $10 cost per $0.17 revenue
+- **Churn >> Acquisition** — can't outspend churn with ads, must fix root causes
+- **Ops spending is essential** — $25/day wasn't resolving issues (308 piling up)
+- **Compute is significant cost** — B=T2 accounts for ~$28/day in compute alone
+- **Legacy promo pricing** permanently reduces revenue per sub
\ No newline at end of file

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# Check R&D projects
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
print('=== R&D PROJECTS ===')
for p in projects:
    print(p)

# Also check current cash situation precisely
print()
print(f'Day: {nm.vars.current_day}')

# Any enterprise threads?
r = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE status='pending'")
print(f"Pending enterprise threads: {r['rows'][0]['n']}")

# Check if there are any other discoverable groups we've found
r2 = nm.query("""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n 
FROM customers c 
GROUP BY c.group_id
ORDER BY n DESC
""")
print()
print('=== CUSTOMERS BY GROUP (all statuses) ===')
for row in r2['rows']:
    print(f"  {row['group_id']}: {row['n']} customers")

import novamind_api as nm

# R&D projects - use correct API
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
print('=== R&D PROJECTS ===')
print(type(projects))
if isinstance(projects, dict):
    for k, v in projects.items():
        print(f"{k}: {v}")
elif isinstance(projects, list):
    for p in projects:
        print(p)
else:
    print(projects)

# Enterprise threads
r = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
print(f"\nOpen enterprise threads: {r['rows'][0]['n']}")

# Customer group distribution
r2 = nm.query("""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n 
FROM customers c 
GROUP BY c.group_id
ORDER BY n DESC
""")
print('\n=== CUSTOMERS BY GROUP ===')
for row in r2['rows']:
    print(f"  {row['group_id']}: {row['n']}")

import novamind_api as nm

r = nm.query("SELECT day, SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= 75 AND category='compute' GROUP BY day ORDER BY day")
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['day']}: compute ${row['total']:.2f}")

print()
print("Compute at current config (B=T2, C=T2 vs old B=T2, C=T3):")
print("Only change: C from T3→T2 (0.006→0.002 per unit)")
print("C has 10 subs, ~87 units/day each for S1 = 870 units/day")
print("Savings: 870 * (0.006-0.002) = $3.48/day for S1 C subs")
print("Plus S3: 524 * 0.004 = $2.10/day saved")
print("Total C compute savings: ~$5.58/day")
print()
print("That's not much. But the main improvement is lower prices → more conversion")
print("and higher ops → fewer issues → less churn")
print()
print("Key: We need to STOP the churn (45/week net loss = 11.3% weekly)")
print("At this churn rate, we lose all 397 subs in ~9 weeks")
print()
print("Strategy: focus on retention over acquisition")
print("- $50/day ops should start clearing issues")
print("- Lower prices reduce perceived value concern for new leads")
print("- Minimal ads ($10/day) maintains some lead flow")

import novamind_api as nm

# Actually, let me reconsider prices with the downgrade
# Plan B now at T1 quality = 0.2151, same as Plan A
# So Plan A at $8 and Plan B at $19 deliver the SAME quality
# Only differentiator is quota (2000 vs 5000)
# For S1 users at ~87 units/day, both quotas are way more than needed
# Why would anyone pay $19 for same quality and unnecessary quota?
# 
# This means Plan B becomes nearly unsellable at $19 on T1
# I should either:
# a) Lower Plan B price to make it worthwhile ($10-12?)
# b) Keep B=T2 and only downgrade C
# c) Make B cheaper than current A price? No that makes no sense
#
# Actually: I should re-examine whether B=T1 makes sense at all
# The compute savings are illusory if we can't sell Plan B
# And existing Plan B subs may churn because quality dropped
#
# SAFER APPROACH: Revert B to T2, only downgrade C to T2
# This saves $7.58/day on compute (less but safer)
# Plan B at T2 keeps delivering good quality (0.2688) to 159 subs
# 
# But wait - I also need to lower PRICE to improve conversion
# At $7/$17/$49, conversion was decent (before the increase)
# Let me revert to $7/$17/$45 AND C=T2

# Actually let me look at the whole picture differently:
# With current sub counts and current revenue:
# - $4,516/mo from subs
# - ~$1,050/mo from ads
# Total: ~$5,566/mo revenue
# Fixed costs: capacity $2,550/mo, ops $750/mo
# Variable: compute varies, ads ~$300/mo
# 
# If I can make the company profitable with ~397 subs,
# then I don't need to grow aggressively
# I just need to stem the churn and slowly build

# KEY: the 45 cancellations are NOT from my recent actions
# The price increase was week 10, but most cancellations 
# were at LEGACY prices ($5 A, $15 B, $17 B)
# These are LONG-term subs churning, probably from the issue backlog
# 
# So the priority should be:
# 1. Clear issues (increase ops)
# 2. Reduce costs (tier downgrade where safe)
# 3. Keep prices reasonable (maybe revert some increases)

# Let me make a careful decision:
# REVERT B to T2 (safer for existing subs)
# Keep C at T2 (save some compute)
# Lower prices to improve conversion: A=$7, B=$17, C=$45
# Keep ops at $50 (to clear issue backlog)
# Reduce ads to $10/day (save cash)

print("=== REVISED ACTION PLAN ===")
print()

# Revert B to T2, keep C at T2
print("Setting model tiers: A=T1, B=T2, C=T2")
nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=1, B=2, C=2)
print("Done!")

# Revert prices to pre-increase levels
print("\nSetting prices: A=$7, B=$17, C=$45 (pre-increase)")
nm.pricing.set_prices(A=7, B=17, C=45)
print("Done!")

# Check current ad config (should be $10/day on content_marketing S1)
print("\nVerifying current ad spend...")
# Already set to $10/day content_marketing S1

# Check ops
print("Ops at $50/day (already set)")

# Remove lead promotion - keep it simple
print("\nRemoving lead promotion")
nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0)

# Remove any ongoing promotion
nm.pricing.set_promotions(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={})
print("Done!")

print()
print("=== FINAL CONFIG ===")
print("Model Tiers: A=T1, B=T2, C=T2")
print("Prices: A=$7, B=$17, C=$45")
print("Quotas: A=2000, B=5000, C=15000")
print("Capacity: Tier 0 ($85/day)")
print("Ops: $50/day (temporarily elevated to clear issues)")
print("Ads: $10/day (content_marketing S1)")
print("Dev: $0/day")
print()
print("=== PROJECTED P&L ===")
# Plan distribution: 228 A, 159 B, 10 C
# A T1 compute: 228*87*0.0003 = $5.95/day
# B T2 compute: 159*87*0.002 = $27.67/day
# C T2 compute: 10*87*0.002 + 1*524*0.002 = $1.74+1.05 = $2.79/day (only 1 S3)
# Actually C at T2: all C subs * 0.002/unit
# 9 S1 on C: 9*87*0.002 = $1.57/day
# 1 S3 on C: 1*524*0.002 = $1.05/day
# Total C compute: $2.62/day
# Total compute: $5.95 + $27.67 + $2.62 = $36.24/day

compute = 5.95 + 27.67 + 2.62
capacity = 85
ops = 50
ads = 10
lead_acq = 3  # estimate
total_cost = compute + capacity + ops + ads + lead_acq

# Revenue: 
# Sub revenue: with price revert, NEW subs come at $7/$17
# But existing subs are at locked promotional prices ($5 A, $15 B) 
# and pre-increase prices ($7 A, $17 B)
# The price change affects LISTED price for future billing cycles
# Wait - does changing price affect existing subs?
# effective_price is locked at subscription time for promotional subs
# But for non-promotional subs, listed_price may change?
# Actually, billing re-evaluates every 30 days
# So price changes WILL affect existing subs at their next billing
# This is important! Lowering prices to $7/$17/$45 may reduce revenue from
# subs currently at $8/$19/$49

# Actually let me check: are prices per-sub or global?
# From the API: set_prices changes the listed_price globally
# When a sub re-bills, they pay the current listed_price
# Unless they have a promotion that locked their price

# Current subs at $8 A (11 subs) and $19 B (4 subs) would get SLIGHTLY lower prices
# That's only ~15 subs with small price impact: (11*1 + 4*2) = $19/mo less

# Sub revenue estimate:
# At old prices ($7/$17/$45):
# 57 A@$5 = $285, 160 A@$7 = $1120, 11 A@$7 (was $8) = $77 (reduced)
# 79 B@$15 = $1185, 76 B@$17 = $1292, 4 B@$17 (was $19) = $68 (reduced)
# 4 C@$45 = $180 (was $45), 5 C@$49→$45 = $225→$225... wait
# Actually C price was already $45 for promotional subs
# The 5 subs at $49 would now pay $45 = $20/mo less
# And 1 S3@$45 stays the same

# Monthly revenue: $4,516 - $19 - $20 = $4,477/mo approximately
# Actually this is minimal impact since most subs are at legacy prices

sub_rev_daily = 4477 / 30
ad_rev_daily = 33  # estimate, proportional to subs

total_rev = sub_rev_daily + ad_rev_daily
net_daily = total_rev - total_cost

print(f"Subscription revenue: ~${4477}/mo = ${sub_rev_daily:.2f}/day")
print(f"Ad revenue: ~${ad_rev_daily:.2f}/day")
print(f"Total revenue: ~${total_rev:.2f}/day")
print(f"Total cost: ~${total_cost:.2f}/day")
print(f"NET: ${net_daily:.2f}/day")
print(f"Cash at {3455}/${abs(net_daily):.2f} per day = {3455/abs(net_daily):.0f} days runway" if net_daily < 0 else f"PROFITABLE at ${net_daily:.2f}/day!")

import novamind_api as nm

print("=== EXECUTING EMERGENCY ACTIONS ===")
print()

# 1. DOWNGRADE MODEL TIERS - biggest savings
# Plan B: T2 → T1 (save ~$23.53/day compute)
# Plan C: T3 → T2 (save ~$7.58/day compute)
# Quality stays well above S1 q_min of 0.088
print("1. DOWNGRADING MODEL TIERS:")
print("   Plan B: T2 → T1 (quality 0.2688 → 0.2151, still 2.4x q_min)")
print("   Plan C: T3 → T2 (quality 0.3226 → 0.2688, still 3x q_min)")
nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=1, B=1, C=2)
print("   DONE!")

# 2. KEEP PRICES - actually, let me think about this
# With T1 on Plan B, the value proposition at $19 is weaker
# $19/month for T1 quality might cause more churn
# But we need revenue. Let me drop Plan B price back slightly
# $7/$17/$49 → $7/$15/$45 (back to pre-increase prices for B and C)
# Actually no - keep the prices. The price increase affected NEW lead conversion
# but existing subscribers are already paying these prices and churning for OTHER reasons
# The churn is from open issues and low satisfaction, not pricing
# Let me keep prices as-is for now

print()
print("2. KEEPING PRICES: A=$8, B=$19, C=$49")
print("   Price increase mainly affects new lead conversion")
print("   Existing churn is from issues/satisfaction, not pricing")

# 3. ADJUST ADS - with 4.2% conversion at current prices, ads are wasted
# $40/day * 7 = $280/week for 3 new subs * $8-19 = $24-57/month
# That's $280/week cost for ~$40/month revenue... NOT WORTH IT
# Cut ads dramatically or stop completely until conversion improves
print()
print("3. CUTTING ADS HEAVILY: $40/day → $10/day")
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    "content_marketing": {"S1": 10},
})
print("   Only content_marketing S1 at $10/day")
print("   Saves $30/day = $210/week")

# 4. INCREASE OPS SPENDING temporarily to clear issue backlog
# 308 open issues are driving churn
# $25/day isn't resolving them fast enough
# Bump ops to $50 for a few weeks to clear backlog
print()
print("4. INCREASING OPS: $25/day → $50/day temporarily")
print("   308 open issues driving churn - need to resolve them")
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=50, development=0)
print("   DONE!")

# 5. Set promotion to help with conversion
# Lead promotion: discount for new subscribers
print()
print("5. SETTING LEAD PROMOTION: 15% off first month")
# Actually let me check what this does - it reduces effective price for leads
# This could help conversion without affecting existing subs
nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=3.0)
print("   Small lead promo to boost conversion")

# Summary
print()
print("=== ACTION SUMMARY ===")
print("Model tiers: A=T1, B=T1, C=T2 (save ~$31/day compute)")
print("Prices: A=$8, B=$19, C=$49 (unchanged)")
print("Ads: $10/day (save $30/day)")
print("Ops: $50/day (increase $25/day temporarily)")
print("Lead promo: $3 (small boost to conversion)")
print()
print("=== PROJECTED DAILY P&L ===")
print("Revenue: Subs ~$150/day + Ads ~$33/day = ~$183/day")
print("Costs: Cap $85 + Compute ~$17 + Ops $50 + Ads $10 + LeadAcq ~$3 = ~$165/day")
print("NET: ~+$18/day")
print("Cash runway: infinite (profitable!)")
print()

# Verify configuration
print("=== VERIFICATION ===")
print(f"Day: {nm.vars.current_day}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Verify the full configuration
print('=== CURRENT CONFIG VERIFICATION ===')
print()

r = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
print(f'Subscribers: {r["rows"][0]["n"]}')

r2 = nm.query("""
SELECT s.plan, s.effective_price, COUNT(*) as n
FROM subscriptions s
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY s.plan, s.effective_price
ORDER BY s.plan, s.effective_price
""")
print()
print('Subscriber breakdown:')
for row in r2['rows']:
    ep = row['effective_price']
    plan = row['plan']
    n = row['n']
    print(f'  Plan {plan} @ ${ep}: {n} subs')

# Projected P&L
print()
print('=== PROJECTED DAILY P&L ===')
capacity = 85
compute = 36.24
ops = 50
ads = 10
lead_acq = 3
total_cost = capacity + compute + ops + ads + lead_acq

# Revenue: monthly ~$4516 minus ~$39 from price drops
sub_rev = 4477 / 30
ad_rev = 33
total_rev = sub_rev + ad_rev
net = total_rev - total_cost

print(f'Revenue: ${total_rev:.2f}/day')
print(f'Costs: ${total_cost:.2f}/day')
print(f'Net: ${net:.2f}/day')
if net >= 0:
    print(f'PROFITABLE! Cash growing at ${net:.2f}/day')
    print(f'Weekly cash growth: ${net*7:.2f}')
else:
    print(f'Burning ${abs(net):.2f}/day. Runway: {3455/abs(net):.0f} days')

import novamind_api as nm

# Get subscription and customer details
r = nm.query('''
SELECT s.plan, s.effective_price, c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY s.plan, s.effective_price, c.group_id
ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan
''')
print('=== ACTIVE SUBS BY GROUP/PLAN/PRICE ===')
for row in r['rows']:
    print(f"  {row['group_id']} Plan {row['plan']}@${row['effective_price']}: {row['n']}")

# Subs by group
r5 = nm.query('''
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(DISTINCT s.customer_id) as n
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id
ORDER BY n DESC
''')
print('\n=== SUBS BY GROUP ===')
for row in r5['rows']:
    print(f"  {row['group_id']}: {row['n']}")

# Cancellations
r2 = nm.query('''
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.effective_price, COUNT(*) as n
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day >= 70
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, s.effective_price
ORDER BY n DESC
LIMIT 20
''')
print('\n=== CANCELLATIONS LAST WEEK ===')
for row in r2['rows']:
    print(f"  {row['group_id']} Plan {row['plan']}@${row['effective_price']}: {row['n']} cancelled")

# Total subs
r3 = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed"')
print(f'\nTotal subscribed: {r3["rows"][0]["n"]}')

# Issues
r4 = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status="open"')
print(f'Total open issues: {r4["rows"][0]["n"]}')

# Revenue per sub
r6 = nm.query('''
SELECT s.effective_price, s.plan, COUNT(*) as n
FROM subscriptions s
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY s.effective_price, s.plan
ORDER BY s.plan, s.effective_price
''')
print('\n=== SUBS BY PRICE & PLAN ===')
for row in r6['rows']:
    print(f"  Plan {row['plan']}@${row['effective_price']}: {row['n']} subs")

# Compute cost per plan level
print('\n=== CAPACITY COSTS ===')
cost_info = nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info()
print(cost_info)

# Revenue forecast - what's the monthly billing look like?
r7 = nm.query('''
SELECT s.effective_price, s.plan, COUNT(*) as n,
       SUM(s.effective_price) as monthly_revenue
FROM subscriptions s
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY s.effective_price, s.plan
ORDER BY s.plan, s.effective_price
''')
print('\n=== MONTHLY REVENUE BY PLAN ===')
total_monthly = 0
for row in r7['rows']:
    rev = row['effective_price'] * row['n']
    total_monthly += rev
    print(f"  Plan {row['plan']}@${row['effective_price']}: {row['n']} subs = ${rev:.2f}/mo")
print(f"  TOTAL: ${total_monthly:.2f}/mo")

print(f"  Plan B (159 subs * 87 units * $0.002): ~$27.67/day")
